# UC-18.1.18 · APIC Cluster Replication Latency

## Description

Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention.

## Value

Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention.

## Implementation

Map fields from your APIC release; some metrics require Cisco DC Networking App. Alert on sustained replication lag.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: APIC `avictrl` / cluster diagnostics API.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map fields from your APIC release; some metrics require Cisco DC Networking App. Alert on sustained replication lag.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_diag" earliest=-24h
| where repl_delay_ms>500 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)split|partition|lag")
| table _time, apic_id, repl_delay_ms, message
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**APIC Cluster Replication Latency** — Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention.

Documented **Data sources**: APIC `avictrl` / cluster diagnostics API. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:cluster_diag. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_diag", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where repl_delay_ms>500 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)split|partition|lag")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **APIC Cluster Replication Latency**): table _time, apic_id, repl_delay_ms, message
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the APIC GUI for the same tenants, EPGs, and managed objects, and the affected fabric nodes.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (repl delay), Table (alerts), Single value (max lag ms).

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_diag" earliest=-24h
| where repl_delay_ms>500 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)split|partition|lag")
| table _time, apic_id, repl_delay_ms, message
| sort -_time
```

Line chart (repl delay), Table (alerts), Single value (max lag ms).
